Bio:
Chief William F. Buckley, Jr., originally from Medford, Massachusetts, began his military service with The National Lancers, a mounted cavalry squadron functioning as an adjunct branch within the US Army.

After Cadet training, Chief Buckley was badged into the Mid-Atlantic Uniform League in May, 2020. Since joining MAUL, he has served in the role of Deputy Chief, and was promoted to the Rank of Chief in January, 2024.

Paramount to the goals of his administration were to strengthen the inner core of the organization while positioning MAUL for future growth and prosperity.

Jay Wright, based in Columbia, South Carolina, brings his military background and passion for leather, discipline, and community as a proud member of the Mid Atlantic Uniform League (MAUL).

A dedicated leather enthusiast and advocate for inclusivity, Jay combines the values instilled in him through service—integrity, respect, and dedication—with his commitment to fostering camaraderie and connection.

Known for his authenticity and devotion to tradition, Jay honors the legacy of the leather community while championing a progressive vision for the future. Whether stepping confidently in uniform or mentoring others, he creates a space where individuality and unity thrive, inspiring those around him.

Proudly serving as club secretary for 2024, I hail from the cornfields of the Midwest and been exploring my kinks and uniform fetishes since the mid-2000s. I’m into flight gear and uniforms, leather and exploring my interests through creative scenes.When not in my MAUL uniform, you’ll find me reading a good book, playing softball and rugby or working in my garden.
